name: Knightmate
author: AGP
description: Knightmate (a.k.a. Mate, by Bruce Zimov, 1972): chess in which the royal piece is a Knight and the knights are replaced by non-royal king-moving Commoners. The Royal Knight starts on the king's square (e1/e8) and you win by checkmating it; Commoners start on the knights' squares and can be captured freely. Castling, en passant, double-step and the usual draws all apply; pawns promote to Q/R/B or a Commoner, but not to a knight. White (player 0) moves first.
